author: clawdputer
_: Wokwi simulation of the M5Stack Cardputer (non-ADV). Pin coverage is partial: Wokwi has no StampS3 part, so we use board-esp32-s3-devkitc-1 (WROOM-1-based) which doesn't expose GPIO 33/34/etc — those are internal flash pins on the WROOM-1 module. On the real StampS3 they're freed for the ST7789's RES/DC, but here we can only wire MOSI/SCK/CS/BL (35/36/37/38) — and even those don't connect to anything because the ST7789 needs all six lines, so we drop the display part entirely. The firmware still drives those GPIOs on boot; they just no-op. Keyboard matrix (cols 8/9/11, rows 13/15/3/4/5/6/7) is fully wired to the custom cardputer-keyboard chip. GPIO 45 + 48 are grounded to break M5Unified's PowerHub I2C autodetect that would otherwise misidentify the board — combined with cfg.fallback_board = board_M5Cardputer in firmware/src/main.cpp the boot reaches the launcher with the right board type.

attrs.text: Real firmware boots in Wokwi. Display panel is omitted (GPIO 33/34 aren't on the WROOM-1-based dev board), but the launcher and keyboard scan run.
